creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces in all three forms (list, map, comprehension).

When a void function result is placed in a list or map literal, the entry is silently removed rather than producing an error.

**Repro:**
fn do_nothing():
    pass

items = [do_nothing(), 1, 2]
print(items)   // [1, 2]   (void element silently dropped)

m = {"a": do_nothing(), "b": 2}
print(m)       // {"b": 2}  (void entry silently dropped)

result = [do_nothing() for x in [1, 2, 3]]
print(result)  // []        (all void results dropped)

This can hide confusing bugs when a function was supposed to return a value but does not.

**Design question:** error on void in a collection literal, or keep the silent-filter behavior.
title: Void values silently dropped from collections
